title: Specify dumpfile via command line?
body:
I am migrating a project from a non-FOSS simulator to iverilog. I can run the testbenches and get my pass/fail output (yay!), but it seems like the _only_ way of specifying the dump file and scope is by adding lines to my test bench. There is no mention at all of dump file path in `man vvp`, and some searching finds a thread from 2009 with people discussing this missing feature (and some 10-year-out-of-date discussion of the necessary changes). It is especially odd that dump _format_ can be specified on the command line, but not dump _path_.

I have never seen a simulator that requires testbench code to be modified to get waveform dumps. In commercial tools, the division of labour is normally that your testbench provides stimulus and assertions, and the tool is your means of observing the internals of your design. Having to add lines to testbenches to support a tool change feels odd.

I also have a need to generate and compare multiple VCDs (or equivalent) from multiple runs of the same testbench, with some memories initialised with different hex files. This does not seem to fit into the existing iverilog flow.

- Am I wrong? I have spent a fair amount of time searching/poking around, but the ideal answer is that I'm an idiot and there is a simple option where you can specify dump path on the command line
- If not, how much code is required to add this? I'm more into RTL than software these days, but with a couple of pointers I could implement this
